metadata.title: Salmon Wallet — Open code. Open ownership.
metadata.description: The open-source, multi-chain crypto wallet. Your keys, your coins, your code. Available on iOS, Android, and as a browser extension.
metadata.ogDescription: The open-source, multi-chain crypto wallet. Your keys, your coins, your code.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy — Salmon Wallet
privacy.description: Privacy policy for Salmon Wallet — how we collect, use, and protect your data.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions — Salmon Wallet
terms.description: Terms and conditions for using Salmon Wallet services.

hero.heading:
Open code.
Open ownership.
hero.subheading: A community-owned wallet built for real decentralization.

chains.heading: Multi-chain by design
chains.subheading: Manage assets across Solana and Bitcoin — including devnets and testnets.

why.heading: Open source is non-negotiable
why.subheading: Closed code is a trust tax. Most wallets solve custody, but not control. Open source is the only way to eliminate hidden power, backdoors, and asymmetric control at the wallet layer. If you can't verify it, you don't own it.

items.title: Decentralization without transparency is theater.
items.description: You don't decentralize power by saying it — you do it by exposing the rules. Open code is the social contract of crypto. Without transparency, users may hold keys while still depending on closed systems that shape access, integrations, and distribution.

items.title: Security improves in public
items.description: Silence weakens systems. Transparency hardens them, forcing resilience. Closed systems fail silently. Open ones fail loudly, and improve.

items.title: If users don't own it, it's not Web3.
items.description: Community ownership is not a roadmap item. It's an architectural decision. Salmon is built for users who want more than access to crypto — they want a wallet layer that can be owned, governed, and evolved in the open.

items.title: Swap & Bridge
items.description: Swap and bridge tokens. Fast and seamless.

items.title: Connect to dApps
items.description: Explore the ecosystem. Directly from your wallet.

items.title: Built to grow with you
items.description: Simple to start. Powerful when you need it.

security.heading: Security by design
security.subheading: Built to protect without asking for trust.

items.title: Keys stay with you
items.description: Your private keys are encrypted on your device. Protected by your password. Never shared. Never accessible to us.

items.title: Privacy by default
items.description: No tracking. No data collection. Your balances, addresses, and activity stay private.

items.title: Open security model
items.description: Security through transparency. Open-source code, reviewed in public. Audited continuously by the community.

items.title: Every change reviewed
items.description: All contributions are inspected. Every pull request meets strict internal standards before becoming part of the protocol.

manifesto.heading: Salmon is public infrastructure.
manifesto.subheading: No permissions. No intermediaries. Just proof.

principles.title: Salmon is open-source.
principles.description: Because closed code creates invisible power.

principles.title: Salmon is community-owned.
principles.description: Because users, not corporations, bear the consequences.

principles.title: Salmon assumes adversaries.
principles.description: Security is earned through exposure, not secrecy.

principles.title: Salmon prioritizes longevity over growth.
principles.description: A protocol that survives hype cycles is more valuable than one that chases them.

principles.title: If Salmon fails, it should fail in public.
principles.description: If it succeeds, it should belong to everyone who backed.

items.question: What is Salmon?
items.answer: Salmon is a self-custodial, open-source crypto wallet built as public infrastructure. It allows you to store, manage, and interact with digital assets across chains — without giving up control.

items.question: Does Salmon have access to my private keys?
items.answer: No. Your private keys are encrypted locally on your device and never leave it. We cannot access them — and we don't want to. If you lose your recovery phrase, no one can recover it for you.

items.question: What makes Salmon different from other wallets?
items.answer: Salmon is fully open-source and community-owned by design. There is no closed backend controlling upgrades or hidden logic. Everything can be inspected, reviewed, and verified.

items.question: Is Salmon really open-source?
items.answer: Yes. All core wallet code is publicly available and reviewed in public. Security improves through transparency, not secrecy. You can inspect the repository on GitHub.

items.question: Can I swap or bridge tokens inside the wallet?
items.answer: Yes. Salmon allows you to swap and bridge tokens directly from the wallet interface, without giving up custody of your assets.

items.question: Does Salmon collect user data?
items.answer: No. We do not track balances, addresses, or personal identifiable information. Privacy is the default. For more details, see our Privacy Policy.

items.question: What happens if Salmon stops operating?
items.answer: Salmon is open-source public infrastructure. Even if the original team disappears, the code remains accessible. The protocol can continue to evolve under community stewardship.

items.question: Is Salmon audited?
items.answer: Salmon has passed an audit by Halborn (sponsored by the Eclipse team) in late 2024. It is also continuously reviewed by the community and internally inspected before any code change is merged. Open review is part of the security model.

blog.heading: From the Blog
blog.subheading: Insights and updates from the Salmon team.

items.title: The Promise of Decentralization Starts With Wallets
items.excerpt: Wallets are the most elemental brick in crypto. They hold identity, value, and the keys that make every on-chain action possible.

items.title: Swimming Through the Blockchain Networks
items.excerpt: Understanding the roles of Mainnet, Testnet, and Devnet is essential for both developers and users.

items.title: Building Bridges
items.excerpt: Salmon partnership with StealthEX to bring In-Wallet-Bridge. Jumping from one blockchain to another has always been difficult.

items.title: Introducing Unified Accounts
items.excerpt: Salmon completed its biggest release jumping from Solana to Multi-chain with unified accounts feature.

items.title: Getting Started with Salmon
items.excerpt: After the FTX issue, we realize again that 'not your keys, not your coins' is real. Custody matters.

items.title: Understanding Self-Custodial
items.excerpt: Custodial, Self-Custodial or Non-Custodial, and how to move around with them.

items.title: Not Just One Wallet
items.excerpt: We recommend setting up 3 different wallets: Everyday, Trading, and HODLer for security purposes.

items.title: It's ON!
items.excerpt: After extensive development, Salmon launches its Web version today with open-source code available.

items.title: Salmon Receives Grant From The Serum Fund
items.excerpt: Salmon receives a Serum Grant as continuation of the Sollet wallet with open-source spirit.

items.title: User Friendly Transfers on Solana
items.excerpt: Salmon Wallet integrates with Solana Name Service for simplified transactions using .sol domain names.

footer.description: Open-source, multi-chain crypto wallet. Your keys, your coins, your code.
